mappings.rationaleAI: Psychology covers cognitive (A.3.a), conative (A.3.b), affective dimensions (A.3.c), dispositional tendencies (A.4), and developmental stages (B).
Hominin evolution addresses evolutionary development of mankind (A.1.c).
Natural selection addresses mankind's continuity with nature (A.1) and evolutionary development (A.1.c).
Shared mammalian capacities address primate commonalities (A.1.a) and phylogenetic continuity (A.1.c).
Primate evolution addresses shared capacities (A.1.a) and evolutionary development (A.1.c).
Heritability debates address genetic endowment (A.2.a) and nature-nurture controversy (A.2).
Inheritance shaping personality addresses genetic endowment (A.2.a) and nature-nurture (A.2).
Distinctive human culture addresses human-specific capacities (A.1.b) and cultural conditioning (A.2.b).
Organism-environment interactions address ecological factors (A.2.b).
Sensory experience addresses cognitive dimension including perception (A.3.a).
Memory processes address cognitive dimension (A.3.a).
Visual perception addresses cognitive dimension through sensation (A.3.a).
Nature of thought addresses cognition (A.3.a) and propositional language (A.1.b).
Reasoning and problem solving address cognition including ideation (A.3.a).
Neural basis of cognition addresses cognitive dimension (A.3.a), and AI comparison addresses emergent problems (A.5).
Brain substrates address cognition (A.3.a), and AI relates to human-machine intelligence comparison (A.5).
Determinism and choice address the conative dimension including voluntary/involuntary (A.3.b).
Evolutionary origins of emotions address the affective dimension (A.3.c).
Historical study of emotions addresses the affective dimension (A.3.c).
Happiness and well-being address the affective dimension (A.3.c).
Intelligence differences address nature-nurture (A.2), reasoning (A.3.a), and dispositional capacities (A.4).
Consciousness addresses cognition (A.3.a), and machine consciousness addresses emergent problems (A.5).
Mind-body relationship addresses behaviour factors (A), cognitive/affective dimensions (A.3), and computer intelligence (A.5).
Nature-nurture problem addresses mankind-nature relationship (A.1) and nature-nurture controversy (A.2).
Linguistic diversity addresses propositional language as distinctively human (A.1.b).
Scientific study of language addresses propositional language (A.1.b).
AI capabilities address comparison of human and artificial intelligence (A.5).
Prenatal development addresses prenatal growth (B.2).
Developmental psychology addresses infancy (B.4) and childhood (B.5).
Adolescent development addresses adolescence (B.6).
Selfhood and agency address conative dimension (A.3.b) and dispositional tendencies (A.4).
Creative expression addresses distinctive human capacities (A.1.b) and cognitive ideation (A.3.a).
Biological clocks address genetic endowment physiological characteristics (A.2.a).
